title: Diplazium dinghushanicum
taxonomy: Diplazium dinghushanicum belongs to the family Athyriaceae, which is a group of ferns. It is a species of fern, specifically classified as a member of the genus Diplazium. The family Athyriaceae is further classified under the order Polypodiales, and the class Polypodiopsida. At the kingdom level, it is classified as Plantae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

intro: Diplazium dinghushanicum, also known as Dinghushan diplazium, is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its reported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory diseases and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Diplazium dinghushanicum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often made into tea, or used as an ingredient in herbal decoctions, which are believed to promote respiratory health and improve immune function.
